transcript.text:
Out of the homemade food mukbangs I did recently,
(Found the No. 1 taste!) I think I enjoyed it the best
("The Mishik" Part 1) The Mishik Master Ramyeon
("The Mishik" Part 2) Jjajangmyeon
("The Mishik" Part 3, today, it's bibimmyeon!) I heard they came out with bibimmyeon so I prepared it
("The Mishik" always satisfied her tastebuds...)
(Will the bibimmyeon do justice...?)
(First, slurp it all up!)
(And beef is here)
(An appetizing visual which everyone knows the taste of...)
(No words needed)
I'm not saying this because it's a paid promotion...
Wow, this bibimmyeon is legendary!
(Ultimate legendary bibimmyeon mukbang, let's begin!)
Hello, everyone
Today,

I heard "Harim The Mishik" released bibimmyeon, so I prepared it
(Master Ramyeon Light Flavor, Spicy Flavor / Jjajangmyeon) I think I've tried all the ramyeon variations of "The Mishik"
I really enjoyed their jjajangmyeon
So I heard they came out with bibimmyeon, which I'm really excited to try out today!
(Let me explain about "Harim The Mishik Bibimmyeon"...) This is the bibimmyeon they just released
"A seasoning pack with 10 kinds of veggies and fruits blended into a broth"
"Mixes with the chewy noodles to create a sweet and refreshing bibimmyeon!"
(Successfully managed to cheat) That's what it says
(Expert on "Harim The Mishik") I know this well!
The ingredients
(You can enjoy "Harim The Mishik" even more!) only contain healthy things, not instant food
(Stop crinkling)
I heard that they only use healthy ingredients, so it's like gourmet
(So shiny...) I received a bunch of them and I'm happy
The one at the back's gonna fall!!
Oh no!

(What a peaceful home mukbang...)
They kneaded the noodles with broth, so it's more flavorful!
Back then, the noodles rarely got soggy
I'm excited for the bibimmyeon too!
(Adding the seasoning!)
(+ "The Mishik Bibimmyeon") I added 8 packs of it
For sides, I'll have beef...
About 800g of it
(The seasoning comes squeezing out!)
Wow, it looks good! I added all 8 of them!
Mix it!
As I was cooking the noodles
and pouring it, I touched it with my hand
and it was so supple!!
(Okay, don't get mad...) What am I saying?
It was so supple, I was so surprised

I'm really excited about the new bibimmyeon
Wow, the noodles are so supple
They're like popping out!
(This kind of bounce cannot be contained...) It's bouncy! Look...
(Mix well while being in awe)
(Complete) Anyway!
Bon appetit!
(So excited) I'm try a bite of the bibimmyeon first!
Actually, the noodles are a bit old
I was worried that they'd stick together because of that
but the noodles aren't choppy at all!
It's not dry, and can you see how supple they are?
Supple
I'll try it
Whoa, that's awesome!
Oh, it's really good!

(So good, she starts inhaling...)
This kind of flavor for ramyeon
is practically a first
It's not like those choppy noodles made with flour
(On another level) It's really supple
(A very fancy kind of noodles) The noodles themselves don't feel like they're made from flour!
I can't taste the flour at all
(I love it...) It's not choppy at all, look
The texture is nothing like flour-made noodles
I love the texture!
(Maintaining its suppleness) I think it's been about 20 minutes since I made them!
It's so good!
(Let's grill meat!) I prepared meat as well, so I'll eat it as a side
Solid
(+Solid fuel grill) I recently bought a mini grill
(Why is she so proud? LOL) My heart's racing!

(The solid fuel goes in!) I'm excited!
(Turn on the heat and let it be!) It turns on right away! It's so quick
Wow, this bibimmyeon is legendary!
(+ Plum, Japanese apricot, Korean pear, apples) I think it's because they added fruit
(+ Garlic, onions, ginger, radish, green onions, chili peppers) It's a bit spicy, sweet,
(Seasoning made with 10 kinds of fruit and veggies and broth) and it's very flavorful!
The aftertaste is very refreshing and clean, should I say?
(Hot) It's already heated up!
(Place beef briskets first) I'll grill it first
(Making mouths water with beef briskets...!)
(The best combination!) This goes really well with beef briskets too!
(The best combination!) The bibimmyeon is sweet and refreshing
I'll take out the beef briskets here
(All she thinks about is food) It'll be so good with thinly sliced pork belly too
Thin meat is the best with bibimmyeon!
Love it!

("The Mishik" receives a hundred "Good Job" stickers) They made it so well!
(Refilling her cup somewhere here)
I prepared orange juice today!
(Fish it out!)
(Inhaling the bibimmyeon in super-speed) I think I'll finish these noodles in a flash
Actually, bibimmyeon is
hard to eat when it's soggy
So a lot of the times, it's harder to eat near the end
But at this rate, I don't think these will go soggy till the end of the show
(The heat power of the solid fuel is amazing) It's the best today!
Not bibimmyeon but
(Master Ramyeon Light Flavor / Spicy Flavor) regular ramyeon
(Master Ramyeon used dried noodles) wasn't fried
(The noodles are savorier) But I guess "The Mishik Bibimmyeon" was fried
It's really good
Should I grill thick meat here too?

There's a mix of sirloin and ribs
I don't know which is which
(She just eats it deliciously...) I'll just grill it
It's perfect!
(Eating the beef brisket she had grilled)
(Literally... a bibimmyeon bomber...)
I'm not saying this because it's a paid promotion...
The bibimmyeon is amazing, seriously!
"The Mishik" is the best!!
("The Mishik" receives a thousand "Good Job" stickers)
Ta-da! Amazing, right?
(Rescue)
(Feeling the bibimmyeon to its core...)
Everyone eats meat and then some bibimmyeon
(Meat is just a side...LOL) But I eat meat in order to eat bibimmyeon!
(Huh? The heat turned off!) The heat is getting weaker!

(Gets a bad feeling trying to switch out the solid fuel)
(Bye)
No!
Aren't these signs of good fortune...?
(Let's go big! "The Mishik Bibimmyeon"!!!) It's nothing bad
(The heat is back) There you go!
(Please save me...)
I'm really curious
When do these get soggy?! LOL
It's almost been an hour since I cooked them
(Perfectly fine) They're still not soggy!
(Restored back to normal)
(The bibimmyeon is already almost gone...)
(A sudden fire began) Oh my, the side of this is burnt!
(It's not the time to laugh..) Oh my! The cutting board!
(Save the cutting board) The cutting board caught on fire too!

(Save the cutting board) This could've been really bad!
(Always be careful of fire...) I'll get rid of this
(Grill all the leftover meat safely!)
(Gather all the bibimmyeon)
(So busy.. LOL)
(Next obstacle is to turn off the heat) How to turn off solid fuel
(While she is looking it up...) Solid fuel...
(The heat is getting hotter...)
(Sizzle)
(What solution did she find?)
(A spoon, suddenly?)
(No, go back LOL)
(Just blow it, and you're fine)
(Another peaceful day of her home mukbang)
(Mukbang resumes)
Whether it's 8 ramyeon or 10 ramyeon,

it's my first time
having the noodles not go soggy until the end when I cook in bulk!
These are still chewy!
(Huh? Where did it go?) I'm almost finished with the bibimmyeon
(The orange juice is almost gone as well)
I was debating if I should eat beef tartare or grilled meat with this,
(She ate it 6 months ago!) but I ate beef tartare with it before
So I grilled the meat today
I think it was a good choice! It goes so well together
(One bibimmyeon, two meat pieces)
(Wait, that spoon...?)
(The spoon that failed putting out the fire)
(A multi-purpose spoon that is useful LOL)
(8 packs of "The Mishik Bibimmyeon" + 800g of meat ALL CLEARED!)
It was such a perfect meal today
Thanks to all the kinds of fruit they added,

it was sweet, refreshing, and very delicious
More than anything, the noodles
are the best!
(Much like magical noodles) I can say that these noodles never go soggy
They were so bouncy till the end
and I was so satisfied with this bibimmyeon!!
I had a great meal once again!
